content.problemStatement:
You are given a rectangular fence with length LLL and width WWW.

The fence has **four sides**, and the total length of the fence is:
2Ă—(L+W)2 \times (L + W)2Ă—(L+W).

You have **exactly enough paint** to paint the entire fence using **three colors**: Red, Green, and Blue.

Let the total available paint of each color be RRR, GGG, and BBB respectively, then:
R+G+B=2Ă—(L+W)R + G + B = 2 \times (L + W)R+G+B=2Ă—(L+W).

Each unit of paint can cover one unit length of the fence.

Painting Cost:


Each side of the rectangle must be painted completely.
A side may be painted using **one or more colors**.
The **cost of painting a side** is equal to the **number of distinct colors used on that side**.
Determine the **minimum total cost** required to paint the entire fence.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing five space-separated integers L,W,R,G,BL, W, R, G, BL,W,R,G,B - denoting the length and width of the rectangle, and total available paint for each color respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer â€” the **minimum total painting cost**.
